properties.name: Siachen Glacier
properties.description: World's highest militarised zone. Both India and Pakistan maintain forward posts. Frequent supply-line incidents and avalanche casualties.

properties.name: Shaksgam Valley
properties.description: Pakistan-administered territory claimed by India, de facto controlled with Chinese infrastructure investment. Disputed by all three nations.

properties.name: Depsang Plains
properties.description: Strategically critical high-altitude plateau in Ladakh. Chinese incursions in 2020 blocked Indian access to traditional patrol points.

properties.name: Galwan Valley
properties.description: Site of June 2020 clash that killed 20 Indian and 4+ Chinese soldiers. Buffer zones now in place following disengagement talks.

properties.name: Siliguri Corridor
properties.description: 22km-wide land corridor connecting Northeast India to the rest of the country. Strategically critical; bordered by Bangladesh, Nepal and Bhutan.

properties.name: Mongla Port
properties.description: Bangladesh's second-largest seaport. China has invested heavily in port infrastructure, raising strategic concerns for India about dual-use potential.

properties.name: Chittagong Port
properties.description: Bangladesh's largest port and economic hub. Significant Chinese port development investment. Indian strategic concern over potential PLA Navy access.

properties.name: Gwadar Port
properties.description: Centrepiece of China-Pakistan Economic Corridor (CPEC). Deep-water port with potential dual civilian-military use. Strategically positioned near Strait of Hormuz.

properties.name: Strait of Hormuz
properties.description: Critical global oil chokepoint. Approximately 20% of global petroleum transits here. Iranian, US, and Pakistani naval assets regularly operate in proximity.
